title: Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training
exclusions: Does NOT cover general awareness training (covered by 22.46.1); focuses on privileged-role-specific training.
evidence: Saved search 'priv_role_training' (weekly) emits PAM-managed accounts whose owner lacks a current specialist certificate.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Security reminders and periodic training for workforce; extends to privileged roles.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA §164.308(a)(5)(ii)(A) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.46.3: Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.46.3' running on LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 12.6.3 annual security awareness training must cover role-specific risks.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI-DSS 12.6.3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.46.3: Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.46.3' running on LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST-800-53 AT-3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.46.3: Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.46.3' running on LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An admin has no specialist training record in the last 365 days; the UC fires the next week.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All admins have a current specialist training record; no alert.

dataSources: LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping. — supplemented by authentication logs, system audit trails, and configuration management database (CMDB) asset inventory exports
spl:
| rest /services/pam/vault-inventory splunk_server=*
| fields user account_type
| where account_type="privileged"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=priv_security_training earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user account_type last_completion
description: Provides a named list of privileged-role owners whose specialist training is overdue — a recurring theme in internal audits of ITGC.
value: Makes the 'role-based training' requirement evidence-backed rather than policy-on-paper.
implementation: (1) Tag PAM vault entries with the owner; (2) map course completions to users in the LMS; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-enrol overdue owners. Indexes required: `index=lms`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=completion`.
visualization: Table of overdue admins, bar chart of departments, single-value 'admins with current specialist training'. Key fields: `account_type`, `last_completion`, `user`, `user]`.
knownFalsePositives:
1. **Authorised compliance operations** — The compliance and governance team performs routine control testing, evidence gathering, and policy reviews that generate legitimate activity matching this use case's detection patterns. These operations are documented in the compliance programme calendar and should be excluded by the compliance-ops service account tag.

2. **Scheduled system maintenance windows** — Approved maintenance activities including patching, upgrades, and configuration changes produce expected control deviations during documented change windows that are pre-approved through the change advisory board (CAB).

3. **External audit and assessment activities** — During periodic audits (internal or external), assessors perform sampling, testing, and evidence collection activities that generate unusual access patterns covered by the engagement letter and audit notification.

4. **Third-party service provider operations** — Contracted service providers performing legitimate operations under signed agreements generate expected activity that may trigger monitoring rules during normal service delivery windows.

**Suppression mechanism:** Maintain a `compliance_exception_register` KV Store lookup containing approved maintenance windows, audit engagement periods, service provider identifiers, and compliance team service accounts. The correlation search joins this lookup and suppresses matches where `exception_status=approved` and the exception remains within its validity period.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Indexes**: `lms` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `§164.308(a)(5)(ii)(A)`. The control objective is: "Evidence that HIPAA §164.308(a)(5)(ii)(A) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.46.3: Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `lms` under `completion`, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


Four distinct operational considerations shape this detection's rollout:


• **Hint 1:** Tag PAM vault entries with the owner. CIM-compliance tagging must be validated end-to-end: a downstream datamodel that does not accelerate because of a missing tag is a silent failure.

• **Hint 2:** map course completions to users in the LMS. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** run weekly. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** auto-enrol overdue owners. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the HIPAA control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search correlates the outer dataset (index=`lms`, sourcetype=`completion`) with a subsearch on `user` across the last year. `type=inner` means the search only returns rows where `user` exists in both datasets. This is the correct choice when the detection requires presence in both sources — a left-join would include orphaned rows that cannot be evaluated against the compliance rule.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.46.3: Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual ]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -365d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.46.3,reg=HIPAA
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=lms sourcetype=completion earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `user`, `account_type`, `last_completion`. Run:

```spl
index=lms sourcetype=completion earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table user, account_type, last_completion
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.46.3: HIPAA evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Table Of Overdue Admins (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Bar Chart Of Departments (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Single-Value 'Admins With Current Specialist Training' (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**HIPAA-specific failure modes:**

• Minimum-necessary principle: access-anomaly detections (excessive chart views, unexpected departments) must NOT flag clinicians accessing records for treatment purposes — §164.502(b) explicitly permits this. Join detections against the clinician's current department and patient's active encounter to avoid generating thousands of false positives on ED doctors seeing admitted patients.

• Break-glass and emergency access (§164.312(a)(2)(ii)): emergency accounts will legitimately bypass normal access controls during downtime events. Maintain a lookup `breakglass_declarations.csv` written by the clinical informatics on-call; correlate every break-glass access against a declared event — un-declared emergency access is the finding.

• Audit log retention (§164.312(b)): HIPAA requires 6 years of audit log retention, longer than most Splunk index default settings. Verify `frozenTimePeriodInSecs >= 189216000` for every index named in an HIPAA UC's search, and that the archive tier (S3, cold storage) is provisioned.

• Covered vs non-covered entity scope: if the search unintentionally pulls data from a non-covered business unit (e.g. a research arm not operating as a HIPAA Covered Entity), findings are noise. Filter using the facility/cost-center lookup that the Privacy Officer maintains, not by index name alone.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see who still needs training so awareness stays current across teams and roles.
